days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring the city and its vibrant food scene

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: Proceed through customs and immigration, then take the Airport Express train to Hong Kong station to enter the city.

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel ICON
timeline.detail: Settle into this luxurious hotel in Tsim Sha Tsui, which features stunning harbor views and modern amenities. Perfect spot to relax after your journey.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Mak Ngan Kee
timeline.detail: Enjoy a delicious plate of wonton noodles at this famous local eatery, known for its rich broth and handmade noodles. A must-try for noodle lovers.

timeline.title: Victoria Harbour Promenade
timeline.detail: Stroll along the promenade and take in the panoramic views of Victoria Harbour. Capture photos of the iconic skyline and enjoy the sea breeze.
timeline.mapQuery: Victoria Harbour Promenade, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Eye Bar
timeline.detail: Indulge in modern Asian cuisine with stunning views of the harbor at this rooftop restaurant, known for its ambiance and great food.
timeline.mapQuery: Eye Bar, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

localTips: Purchase an Octopus card at the airport for easy travel on public transport.
Try to catch the Symphony of Lights show at 20:00 along the harbor.
Take the MTR for fast commuting; trains come every few minutes.

days.title: Exploring Central and Lantau Island
days.subtitle: History and breathtaking scenery

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a hearty Hong Kong-style breakfast at this local hotspot, famous for its toast and scrambled eggs.
timeline.mapQuery: Australia Dairy Company, Jordan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Tian Tan Buddha
timeline.detail: Take the Ngong Ping 360 cable car to reach the stunning Tian Tan Buddha on Lantau Island. Enjoy the scenic views during the ride.
timeline.mapQuery: Tian Tan Buddha, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Po Lin Monastery
timeline.detail: Enjoy a vegetarian meal at the famous Po Lin Monastery, offering traditional vegetarian dishes that are refreshing and delicious.
timeline.mapQuery: Po Lin Monastery, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Ngong Ping Village
timeline.detail: Stroll through Ngong Ping Village, where you can find shops and enjoy the beautiful scenery. Don’t miss the chance to take memorable photos.
timeline.mapQuery: Ngong Ping Village, Lantau Island, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Return to Central and visit Man Mo Temple
timeline.detail: Head back to Central and visit the historic Man Mo Temple, dedicated to the gods of literature and war. Appreciate the intricate architecture.

timeline.title: Dinner at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Finish your day with a meal at Mak's Noodle, renowned for its authentic Hong Kong wonton noodles, a true culinary experience you shouldn’t miss.

localTips: The Ngong Ping 360 can get busy; try to go early to avoid long lines.
Bring a light jacket for the cable car ride, as it can get windy.
Check out local shops around Ngong Ping for unique souvenirs.

days.title: Cultural Day in Kowloon
days.subtitle: Markets and local flavors

timeline.title: Breakfast at DimDimSum Dim Sum Specialty Restaurant
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional dim sum breakfast, offering a variety of dumplings and buns, a true Hong Kong culinary delight.
timeline.mapQuery: DimDimSum Dim Sum, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Experience the cultural atmosphere at Wong Tai Sin Temple, where you can see locals practicing their beliefs and performing rituals.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Kowloon Walled City Park
timeline.detail: Visit this historical park that was once a fortified enclave, offering lush gardens and historical information about its past.
timeline.mapQuery: Kowloon Walled City Park, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Savor an affordable Michelin-starred meal at Tim Ho Wan, known for its baked buns with BBQ pork. Get there early to avoid waiting.
timeline.mapQuery: Tim Ho Wan, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Shopping at Mong Kok Market
timeline.detail: Wander around Mong Kok’s vibrant markets, particularly the Ladies' Market and the Flower Market, full of colorful goods and lively atmosphere.

timeline.title: Dinner at Sister Wah
timeline.detail: Enjoy beef brisket noodles at Sister Wah, a local favorite known for its rich broth and tender meat, perfect to end your culinary adventure.

localTips: Bring cash, as some establishments may not accept credit cards.
Haggle at street markets for better prices.
Take advantage of MTR for quick travel within Kowloon.

hotels.name: Hotel ICON
hotels.note: Luxurious hotel with harbor views, close to train station.
hotels.address: 17 Science Museum Rd, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

budget.item: Flights from Penang to Hong Kong

budget.note: Budget for meals during the trip

urgent.label: Book Airport Express Train
urgent.note: Pre-book for the best rates and availability.
